home-assistant/core#106610.body:
### The problem

Water Heater entities exposed to HomeKit do not let you change the operation_mode, the only option in the Home App is "Heat".

Using the Demo Platform water heater as an example, but I've tried it with other Water-Heater entities and I am experiencing the same results.

**Example Operation Modes available in the UI**
![Screenshot 2023-12-28 at 1 54 01 PM](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/assets/10733802/ef95daf4-6092-48cc-8710-e93f420b08e0)

**Only mode shown in the Home App is "Heat"**
![Screenshot 2023-12-28 at 1 54 27 PM](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/assets/10733802/5e96f9f2-427e-46fc-ada0-c73687867a85)


### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2023.12.4

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

_No response_

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

HomeKit Bridge

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/homekit/

### Diagnostics information

[config_entry-homekit-0c05923bfda8f0eeba2b856b73cc548a.json.txt](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/files/13789691/config_entry-homekit-0c05923bfda8f0eeba2b856b73cc548a.json.txt)


### Example YAML snippet

```yaml
configuration.yaml

demo:
water_heater:
  platform: demo
```


### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?

_No response_

### Additional information

For what it's worth, in comparing line 48 in core/homeassistant/components/homekit/type_thermostats.py to other water-heater integrations, I noticed that there were some **items** that I thought might be missing including (but not limited to):
 
from homeassistant.components.water_heater import (
    DOMAIN as DOMAIN_WATER_HEATER,
    SERVICE_SET_TEMPERATURE as SERVICE_SET_TEMPERATURE_WATER_HEATER,
    **STATE_ECO,
    STATE_ELECTRIC,
    STATE_GAS,
    STATE_HEAT_PUMP,
    STATE_HIGH_DEMAND,
    STATE_PERFORMANCE,
    WaterHeaterEntity,
    WaterHeaterEntityFeature,**
)

home-assistant/core#163460.body:
### The problem

As part of the OAuth 2.0 error handling improvements introduced in 2026.3, the OAuth helper now raises new exceptions instead of propagating `aiohttp.ClientResponseError` directly when a token request or refresh fails.
The Google Mail integration uses application credentials but does not use a Data Update Coordinator. This means it will not automatically benefit from the new error handling and needs to be updated manually.

Details on how to migrate can be found in the [blog post](https://developers.home-assistant.io/blog/2026/02/19/oauth-token-request-error-handling#migration).


### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

2026.3

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

_No response_

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

Google Mail

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/google_mail/

### Diagnostics information

_No response_

```yaml
Parent task: https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues/163456
```

```txt

```

home-assistant/core#164573.body: ### The problem I have a media player card for my Sonos One that is set to only be visible if the state of the Sonos One entity is not "Idle". This used to work normally, but then I noticed, particularly after disconnecting from Spotify Connect, that the entity stays stuck in a "Paused" state, even though "No content" is being shown in the Sonos app. The entity is stuck with the "Spotify Connect" source but no song title. Looking at the integration source, it seems that by checking if the title is None, it should assume Idle, but this doesn't seem to be working. ### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue? core-2026.2.3 ### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core? _No response_ ### What type of installation are you running? Home Assistant OS ### Integration causing the issue Sonos ### Link to integration documentation on our website https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/sonos/ ### Diagnostics information [home-assistant_sonos_2026-03-02T09-16-41.521Z.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25678959/home-assistant_sonos_2026-03-02T09-16-41.521Z.log) ### Example YAML snippet ```yaml device_class: speaker supported_features: 4127295 group_members: - media_player.living_room volume_level: 0.61 is_volume_muted: false media_content_id: >- x-sonos-vli:RINCON_542A1BFBBF7601400:2,spotify:1100adcea7116acf3f6585a904217210 media_content_type: music source: Spotify Connect shuffle: false repeat: "off" queue_position: 1 friendly_name: Living Room queue_size: 1 ``` ### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us? ```txt ``` ### Additional information The state of my affected Sonos media player entity is above in the YAML section. Additionally, the activity logs seem to show that it does attempt to switch to the "Idle" state when it is determined to be Idle, only to immediately switch to "Paused" right after.

home-assistant/core#165039.body:
### The problem

## Problem description
After a phase count adjustment, Home Assistant temporarily reports the phase count as "0" until a next API poll from NRGKick is done.

This behaviour doesn't depend on whether the value is set from the UI or via a script(I use a python script to adjust the current & phase count based on the a PV surplus production).
From what I can observe via the NRGKick app, the phase count is actually correctly set and it's just a temporarily incorrectly reported value in Home Assistant after the change was done.

## Reproduction steps:
1. Open the phase count adjustment window (e.g. click on the Phase count in NRGKick device details)
1.  Adjust the value, e.g. from 3 to 1, or from 1 to 3.
1. Observer behaviour: Phase count has changed to "0".
1. Either after a second click to adjust to the desired value or after 30 seconds(default NRGKick API poll), the value changes to the selected phase count from previously being "0".

## Expected behaviour
Value doesn't change to "0" and the correct phase count is shown immediately in Home Assistant.

See attached video for more details:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/90e299f5-1012-44d1-9c59-04c9be7623c4

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2026.3.1

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

_No response_

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

nrgkick

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/nrgkick

### Diagnostics information

I tried this but there's basically nothing helpful in the logs, the only relevant information was `Discovered NRGkick device:...` and `Finished fetching nrgkick data in 0.159 seconds (success: True)`.

### Example YAML snippet

```yaml

```

### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?

```txt

```

_No response_

home-assistant/core#166935.body:
### The problem

The SMHI (Swedish Meteorological and Hydrological Institute) integration has stopped updating weather data. This is due to the provider officially deprecating and shutting down the pmp3g API today, March 31, 2026.

The current integration (and likely the underlying smhi-pkg library) is hardcoded to use the deprecated pmp3g endpoints for forecasts. SMHI has replaced this with their new SNOW1gv1 API for forecasts and Mesan2gv2 for analysis data. The integration needs to be refactored to use the new endpoints to restore functionality.

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2026.3.4

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

core-2026.3.4

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

smhi

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/smhi/

```

home-assistant/core#166976.body:
### The problem

Starting from Home Assistant 2026.4.Home Assistant Core (venv installation) fails to start because it incorrectly loads the hassio integration during bootstrap.

This should never happen in a Core installation without Supervisor.

As a result, Home Assistant tries to import IngressPanel from aiohasupervisor.models, but this class has been removed in recent versions of aiohasupervisor, causing a fatal error at startup.

Investigating between versions 2026.3.4 and 2026.4.b*, I discovered that the hassio/addon_panel.py module imports supervisor functions that aren't used in core. (from aiohasupervisor.models import IngressPanel)

I understand that core is deprecated but is my favorite install to controll my  raspberry.

I've tested two solutions successfully.
1) Modify the addon_panel.py code with:
try:
from aiohasupervisor.models import IngressPanel
except Exception:
IngressPanel = None

2) Another solution without modifying the code is to create a custom_component/hassio with an empty __init__.py
This avoids loading hassio and I can use HA in venv even for 2026.4. 


### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

System Information version | core-2026.4.0b7 -- | -- installation_type | Home Assistant Core dev | false hassio | false docker | false container_arch | null user | homeassistant virtualenv | true python_version | 3.14.3 os_name | Linux os_version | 6.12.75+rpt-rpi-v8 arch | aarch64 timezone | Europe/Rome config_dir | /home/homeassistant/.homeassistant

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

2026.3.4

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant Core

### Integration causing the issue

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

```

home-assistant/core#167072.body:
### The problem

## The problem

The Proxmox VE integration's backup status binary sensor (`binary_sensor.<node>_backup_status`) always reports "Problem" even when backups complete successfully.

## Root cause

The `STATUS_OK` constant in `const.py` is set to `"ok"` (lowercase), but the Proxmox API returns `"OK"` (uppercase) in the task status field. The case-sensitive comparison in `binary_sensor.py` (`data.backups[0]["status"] != STATUS_OK`) therefore always evaluates to `True`, causing the sensor to permanently indicate a problem.

The test fixture at `tests/components/proxmoxve/fixtures/nodes/tasks.json` already correctly has `"status": "OK"` (uppercase), and the test snapshot encoded the buggy behavior by expecting `'state': 'on'`.

## Environment

- Home Assistant (dev branch)
- Proxmox VE 8.x
- Integration: proxmoxve

## Steps to reproduce

1. Set up the Proxmox VE integration
2. Run a backup (vzdump) that completes successfully (`TASK OK`)
3. Observe the backup status binary sensor reports "Problem"

## Fix

PR #167069

### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2026.4.0

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

_No response_

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

proxmoxve

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/proxmoxve/

### Diagnostics information

_No response_

```yaml

```

```txt

```

_No response_

home-assistant/core#167191.title: Arcam FMJ Receivers Integration stopped working after upgrade to HA 2026.4.0

home-assistant/home-assistant.io#43515.body:
### Feedback

I wanted to comment about how grateful I am for finding this integration!  I live on 15 acres and need Internet connectivity in some of the buildings that are over 0.5 KM from my house.  I've been using the Ubiquiti AirMax AP and LightBeam stations for over four years now.  I'm not using UISP to manage them so having this integration to be able to quickly see the status of all of my long distance radios in one place is great!  Here are the devices I'm using:
Access Point: AirMAX LiteAP GPS (x1)
Stations: AirMax LiteBeam AC Gen 2 (x4)

The only suggestions I have currently are that it would be nice to be able to manage firmware like I can from the Unifi Network integration. And, a device reboot button would be a big help.

Great job on this integration!  Thank you!

-James


### URL

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/airos/

### Version

2026.2.1
